SEARCH
Package cpdt.backend.mqtt

Class DeviceStatusPublisher

java.lang.Object
cpdt.backend.mqtt.DeviceStatusPublisher
@Component public class DeviceStatusPublisher extends Object
Publishes device status updates to the MQTT broker.

This component establishes an MQTT connection and publishes device status messages generated by the backend. Published messages notify external systems, including the simulator, whenever a device's operational status changes.

The publisher manages the MQTT client lifecycle, including connection establishment and graceful shutdown.

Since:
1.0
Author:
Lakshmi Sanjeev

    Creates a publisher for device status messages.

  • Constructor Details

    • DeviceStatusPublisher

      public DeviceStatusPublisher(@Value("${mqtt.broker}") String brokerUrl, @Value("${mqtt.client-id}") String clientId, @Value("${mqtt.qos}") int qos) throws org.eclipse.paho.client.mqttv3.MqttException
      Creates a publisher for device status messages.
      Parameters:
      brokerUrl - the MQTT broker URL
      clientId - the MQTT client identifier
      qos - the Quality of Service level for published messages
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.paho.client.mqttv3.MqttException - if the MQTT client cannot be created

  • Method Details

    • connect

      @PostConstruct public void connect() throws org.eclipse.paho.client.mqttv3.MqttException
      Establishes a connection to the MQTT broker.

      This method is automatically invoked during component initialization to ensure the publisher is ready before messages are transmitted.

      Throws:
      org.eclipse.paho.client.mqttv3.MqttException - if the broker connection cannot be established

    • publish

      public void publish(DeviceStatusMessage message)
      Publishes a device status update to the MQTT broker.

      The supplied status message is serialized into JSON and published to the device-specific MQTT topic using the configured Quality of Service (QoS) level.

      Parameters:
      message - the device status message to publish

    • shutdown

      @PreDestroy public void shutdown()
      Closes the MQTT connection and releases associated resources.

      This method is automatically invoked during application shutdown to disconnect the MQTT client gracefully.
